cultural_lineage_roots: Fuses West African hunter tracking patterns with traditional Cameroonian dance openings.
movement_mechanics: Stand with feet hip-width apart, keeping your core tight and hands anchored at your chest center.
Take a large controlled step backward with your right leg, lowering your hips until your back knee is hovering just above the ground.
As you drop into the lunge, explosively extend both arms straight out to your sides, opening your chest wide to emulate a lion tracking prey.
Drive through your front heel to return to the starting standing position while snapping your hands back to your chest center.
Alternate legs on the next rep and repeat fluidly, maintaining continuous control.
audio_coaching_cues: Open the chest wide as you step back.
Keep your weight anchored firmly in your front heel.
Exhale sharply as you explode back to a standing position.

cultural_lineage_roots: Derived directly from the core stability conditioning drills of ancient Chinese martial arts.
movement_mechanics: Assume a strict high plank position, tracking your hands directly beneath your shoulders with your feet pressed close together.
Lower your entire torso toward the ground in a rigid line, keeping your elbows tucked tightly against your ribs to focus energy on the triceps.
Push through your palms to ascend, and as your arms reach full extension, explosively lift your right hand to tap your left shoulder.
Place your hand back down, lower into your next push-up rep, and tap your right shoulder with your left hand at the peak.
Keep your hips perfectly level throughout the entire set, resisting any side-to-side torso rocking.
audio_coaching_cues: Lock your core tight like a wooden board.
Keep your elbows tucked into your ribs; don't let them flare.
Stabilize your hips completely before you tap your shoulder.
